are are however used in scholarly studies vaishnavism shaivism shaktism and smartism these are sometimes referred as denominations of hinduism they differ in primary deity at the centre of each tradition notable feature of hindu denominations is they not deny other concepts the divine or deity and often celebrate as henotheistic equivalent denominations of hinduism states lipner are unlike those found major major religions of the world because hindu denominations are fuzzy with individuals practising more than and he suggests term hindu polycentrism although hinduism contains many denominations and philosophies it is linked by shared concepts recognisable rituals cosmology shared textual resources pilgrimage sacred sites and questioning authority typology the word hindu is exonym this word hindu is derived the indo-aryan and sanskrit word sindhu sindhu which means large body of water covering river ocean was used as name of indus river and also referred tributaries the actual term hindu' first occurs states gavin flood as persian geographical term the people who lived beyond the river sanskrit sindhu hindus are persons who regard themselves as culturally ethnically or religiously adhering to aspects of hinduism historically the term has also been used as geographical cultural and later religious identifier for people living indian subcontinent in th century european merchants colonists began to refer to followers indian religions collectively hindus until about mid century hindus subscribe diversity of ideas spirituality traditions have no ecclesiastical order no religious authorities no governing body no prophet nor any binding holy book hindus can choose be polytheistic pantheistic monotheistic monistic agnostic or humanist hinduism as it commonly known can be subdivided major currents historical division six darsanas philosophies philosophies two schools vedanta and are currently most prominent classified primary deity or deities four major hinduism modern currents are vaishnavism vishnu shaivism shiva shaktism shakti smartism five deities treated as same these deity-centered denominations synthesis various philosophies such as samkhya yoga vedanta as well as shared spiritual concepts as moksha dharma karma samsara ethical precepts such as texts upanishads
